Subscribers onlyLiz Truss and Rishi Sunak: Who are the two finalists to succeed Boris Johnson?                The members of the Conservative Party have a month to decide between the foreign secretary and the former chancellor of the Exchequer.

Subscribers onlyNovember 13 attacks trial: Questions raised by the verdict  Eleven defense lawyers questioned the court's June 29 verdict for the November 13 attacks in France in a 'Le Monde' op-ed that scandalized many of those involved in the trial. However, their position fuels the debate on three major points.

James Webb telescope may have found most distant known galaxy  Known as GLASS-z13, it dates back to 300 million years after the Big Bang, about 100 million years earlier than anything previously identified.

ECB surprises markets with half-point rate hike  The bank’s surprise hike of half a percentage point for the 19 countries using the euro currency is expected to be followed by another increase in September.

Long Read   Anthony Hernandez and  Eric Collier The Nike saga: When jogging became a fad 'The Nike saga' (2/5). In the early 1970s, the runner Steve Prefontaine, Nike's brand ambassador, helped to popularize running.   7 min read

Investigation   Ivanne Trippenbach Storm brews in Eric Zemmour's far-right party after crushing defeats Plagued by internal criticism, shunned by its members and competing with the RN, the far-right Reconquête! party is facing turbulence. Its leaders hope to relaunch in the autumn.   5 min read

Background   Pierre Barthélémy Subscribers onlyThe year the mystery of the hieroglyphs became clear '1822. Hieroglyphs Deciphered' (4/5). Despite competition from the British polymath Thomas Young, it is Champollion and his passion for languages who should get the credit for decoding ancient Egyptian writing – making the study of an entire civilization possible.   5 min read
